title: The Hollow Forest
author: Andrew Hilton
introduction:
Three weeks ago, Marek sent his last message from the eastern forest: “I think I’ve found the cause.” He was a territorial warden — official access, methodical notes, the kind of man who logged everything. Then nothing.

You’ve been sent to find him. The forest on the empire’s eastern margin is where you start. You have his last known position, a survey map, and a hunting knife. What you don’t have is any certainty about what you’re walking into.

The forest looks wrong. You can’t say exactly how. Not yet.

title: Black Flag Running
author: Andrew Hilton
introduction:
Nassau stinks of low tide and ambition. You smell it before you see the port — tar and fish and something underneath, a particular sweetness that means too many people living too close together without enough to lose.

The Adamant slips into harbour on the morning watch, and the first thing you see is not the fort or the market or the long row of ships stripped to their ribs — it is the three Crown warships sitting at the mouth of the bay, close enough that you can read their names. Crown's Justice. Fervent. Resolve. Three ships to Nassau's none. Commodore Ashford is patient. He has been here eleven days, your harbour pilot tells you. He has not fired a shot.

He is waiting for something.

You have come to Nassau for work. What you have arrived into is something larger — a free republic counting the hours until it stops being free, a sunken ship with secrets that could end a career or start a war, and four factions pulling in four directions with Nassau's future between them.

The Adamant's crew is watching you. The harbour is watching you. Somewhere above the waterline, the town is already deciding what kind of captain you are.

title: Tide of the Leviathan
author: Andrew Hilton
introduction:
The storm took your ship three days ago. The Windborne went down in minutes — one moment you were fighting the sails, the next you were in black water, choking on brine, watching the mast disappear beneath the waves.

You washed ashore at Saltmere, a grey port town clinging to cliffs above the Sunken Reaches — a vast stretch of shallow reef and drowned ruins where the sea swallows ships whole. The locals pulled you from the surf, fed you, and told you something that turned your blood cold.

The storm was not natural. Beneath the Reaches, something ancient stirs — a being the fisherfolk call the Leviathan. A cult of zealots, the Drowned Priests, has been performing rituals to awaken it fully. When the tide comes in tonight, they will complete the final rite.

Your crew is missing. Your ship is gone. But you still have your cutlass and three days' worth of provisions. Your adventure begins at the Saltmere harbour.

title: The Crypt of Count Valdric
author: Andrew Hilton
introduction:
The village of Ashford is dying. For three months its crops have withered in the fields and its people have sickened with a wasting fever that no healer can cure. The village elder, Maren — a sharp-eyed woman who remembers the old stories — knows the cause.

A century ago, the necromancer Count Valdric ruled these lands from the fortress now known as Valdric Keep. When the people finally rose against him, they sealed his corpse in the crypt beneath the ruins with a signet ring of binding. But the ring has been lost — stolen, perhaps, or shaken loose by the tremors that struck last autumn — and without it, Valdric's death-curse bleeds freely into the land.

Maren has hired you to descend into the crypt, recover the Signet Ring, and reseal the tomb before Ashford withers entirely. She presses a vial of holy water into your hand as you leave. 'You will need this,' she says. 'The dead do not rest easily beneath Valdric Keep.'

Your adventure begins on the road to the ruins.

title: The Scavenger of New Babylon Station
author: Andrew Hilton
introduction:
New Babylon Station hangs in the dark between stars, a vast ring of corroded steel and failing systems at the ragged edge of Imperial space. Once it was a trade hub. That was before the war. Before the Empire pulled its garrison and left the station to rot.

Now it is a place for people who have nowhere else to go. Smugglers, refugees, deserters, opportunists. People like you.

You are a scavenger. Three days ago, a dying Qeth trader pressed a data chip into your hand containing coordinates for something called the Void Compass — a Precursor navigation device worth more than the station itself. Inquisitor Malachar of the Imperial Bureau of Antiquities has already docked his corvette. The clock is running.

Navigate the station's factions. Decide who to trust. Descend to the lower decks and find the Compass before Malachar's soldiers do. Eleven possible endings — from legend to ruin — depending on the choices you make and the allies you keep.

Your adventure begins in the Freeport Market on New Babylon's middle ring.

title: The Throne of the Hollow King
author: Andrew Hilton
introduction:
You were a knight once. That ended at Kelmarsh Bridge, where you chose mercy over orders and let the rebel lord's children flee. Your commander stripped your rank, your name, your lands. You have wandered since — hedge-knight, sellsword, nobody.

Now you stand at the edge of Carragh's Foot, a village huddled in the shadow of Mount Aldric. The mountain is wrong. Its peak glows faintly violet against the evening sky, and the air tastes of iron and old grief. Within the ancient palace carved into the mountain's heart, a cult called the Witnesses of the Returning Crown seeks to raise the tyrant-sorcerer Aldric the Undying from death.

The village elder has offered you everything the village can spare: a sword, provisions, and the promise that if you stop the ritual, your name will be remembered. Not your old name. A new one.

It is not much. But it is more than anyone has offered you in a long time.
